behavior_summary: When the game triggers the 'U8P_DEMON::look' (examine) process for this NPC/object, the script emits a single short bark line reading "daemon ".
Immediately after emitting the bark the process suspends or ends; there are no menus, branches, or external calls.
This script functions purely as an examination/short-description handler and does not modify global state or interact with other systems.
notable_control_flow: Entry via the U8P_DEMON::look process (processType 0x0208) → emit one-line Bark ('daemon ') → suspend/terminate the process.
open_questions: The bark text contains a trailing space ('daemon '); is that intentional (formatting/concatenation point) or an artifact of disassembly?
There are two low-confidence Unknown nodes in the decompiled output; one appears to be an opening block and the other a suspend. Are there any hidden side effects in those Unknown nodes?
Is this entity intended to be a purely flavor/descriptive object or could it be expanded later to include dialogue/interaction hooks?

main_function.name: U8P_DEMON::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description response when the player examines this NPC/object by emitting a single bark and ending the U8P_DEMON::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the 'U8P_DEMON::look' (examine) process for this NPC/object

function_map.name: U8P_DEMON::look
function_map.role: examination/description handler (emits a short bark then suspends)
